title: Work in Progress . . .
subtitle: Design of a software distribution platform
description1:
Software distribution platform inspired by a store-like model, currently under development.
Behind a deliberately simple V1, the project is built on an architecture already designed to evolve.
description2: A large part of the work focuses on structuring the project and modeling the data, with particular attention to consistency and scalability.

details.section1Text1: This project is a software distribution platform inspired by Steam, currently under development.
details.section1Text2: Although the first public version will remain simple, the architecture has been designed from the outset to support future evolution and prevent the first release from becoming a limitation for later versions.
details.section2Text1: My role in this project goes beyond development.
details.section2Text2: I am involved in both the technical and organizational aspects of the project, including requirements analysis, version planning, task prioritization, documentation, and the design of the platform's future workflows.
details.section2Text3: I am responsible for the overall structuring of the project, without being the original creator of the product.
details.section2Text4: The functional requirements are defined upfront, and my role is to analyze them, structure them, and translate them into technical solutions.
details.section3Text1: A significant part of the initial work focused on understanding the product model in order to build an architecture that goes beyond the scope of the first public release.
details.section3Text2: The future administration interfaces were designed early in the project.
details.section3Text3: Each form helped me think through entity relationships, data organization, business rules, and the responsibilities of each endpoint before they were implemented.
details.section3Text4: This analysis also led me to split the administration area into several independent sections in order to reduce complexity, simplify future evolution, and maintain a clear separation of responsibilities.
details.section4Text1: An initial phase consisted of defining a data dictionary (field identification, typing, and documentation) as well as a relational diagram.
details.section4Text2: PostgreSQL acts as the source of truth, while the TypeORM entities explicitly reflect the database structure. Every relationship, constraint, enum, composite key, and exception is documented to remain understandable over time.
details.section4Text3: The data model intentionally goes beyond the needs of the first version to avoid having to rethink its architecture whenever a new feature is introduced.
details.section5Text1: I implemented different DTO strategies depending on their purpose.
details.section5Text2: Read DTOs are intentionally broad to quickly cover the data model. In contrast, CREATE and UPDATE DTOs are much stricter and are only introduced when a real application need exists.
details.section6Text1: On the service layer, I chose to move from the simplest cases to the most complex ones.
details.section6Text2: The first implementations focused on read-only reference tables. More advanced processing was developed as the project analysis progressed.
details.section6Text3:
The architecture is built around multiple service layers. 
Some services are exposed through the API, while others remain internal or are only involved as part of a broader orchestration. This approach distributes responsibilities across specialized services instead of concentrating all business logic in a single service.
details.section6Text4: The most complex operations rely on transactions to guarantee data consistency whenever multiple entities are involved. Some processes also integrate caching, batching, or request coalescing whenever they help reduce unnecessary queries and improve scalability.
details.section7Text1: The platform is designed to eventually allow organizations to publish their own products.
details.section7Text2: The project includes a moderation system to control content before publication.
details.section7Text3: Before a resource can be submitted for moderation, an automatic validation layer ensures that all required prerequisites are met. The applied rules vary depending on the type of resource and ensure that all required information is present.
details.section8Text1:
A significant part of the work also focuses on technical documentation.
Throughout the development process, I defined conventions covering DTOs, controllers, services, security, and various architectural decisions. I also created several inventories listing services, their methods, responsibilities, and the different DTOs.
details.section8Text2:
Beyond this project, this documentation helps me improve my own development conventions.
With each project, they evolve, become more refined, and raise my standards for software design and code quality.
details.section9Text1: With the backend now complete, the current phase consists of running a final round of tests across the entire API.
details.section9Text2: These final tests also serve to populate the platform with its first data.
details.section9Text3: As the V1 does not yet include an administration interface, products are created through the API routes in order to go through the same validations, business rules, and processing that the future administration interface will rely on.
details.section9Text4: This approach makes it possible to validate the application's behavior while building the catalog that will be used when the first version is deployed.
